query_group: Cantonese dative verbs; double-object order; predicate class
candidate_source: Li and Lee, Syntactic Distribution of the Semantic Classes of Dative Verbs in English and Cantonese
decision_reason: Direct modern Cantonese examples, acceptability contrasts, verb-class discussion, and an explicit constructional analysis are recoverable.
access_and_locator_state: Full PDF verified; pp. 628-639; proposition locators pending extraction.
independence_scope: One author team and primarily author judgments/theory.
limitations: Does not by itself establish population frequency, all-speaker judgments, or a Canto Span node.

query_group: inverted double-object; dative marker; discourse completeness
candidate_source: Tang, On the 'Inverted' Double Object Construction
decision_reason: Direct examples and explicit comparisons among V-DO-bei-IO, V-IO-DO, and V-DO-IO; includes verb-class, prosodic, speaker-variation, and completeness judgments.
access_and_locator_state: Author PDF and OCR verified; chapter pp. 35-52; exact proposition locators pending extraction.
independence_scope: One theoretical account with author/informant judgments.
limitations: Theory-dependent; some examples explicitly vary by speaker and must not become categorical rules.

query_group: double-object versus oblique; preposition deletion; source/goal
candidate_source: Xu and Peyraube, On the Double Object Construction and the Oblique Construction in Cantonese
decision_reason: Full text provides modern examples, order contrasts, lexical/thematic restrictions, focus/weight discussion, and a preposition-deletion analysis with counterarguments.
access_and_locator_state: Full author-uploaded text verified; Studies in Language 21(1):105-127; DOI verified.
independence_scope: Independent evidence family, while citing earlier Tang work.
limitations: 1990s judgments plus historical evidence; authors acknowledge that some arguments/examples are not decisive.

query_group: heavy theme; dative processing; order variants
candidate_source: Cheung, The Cantonese Dative Constructions: Implications for Processing
decision_reason: Slides expose elicitation counts, processing methods, heavy-theme alternatives, and full examples.
access_and_locator_state: 40-slide PDF verified; slide-level locators pending extraction.
independence_scope: Independent experiment/presentation; not peer-reviewed article text.
limitations: Elicited production and processing do not establish natural conversational prevalence; presentation format limits methodological detail.

candidate_source: Zhang Min, 粵語雙賓語句式的結構和來源
decision_reason: Official one-page abstract states the target contrast and proposes a historical/dialectal origin, useful as a bounded competing account.
access_and_locator_state: Official CUHK abstract verified; one page.
independence_scope: Independent author, but abstract only.
limitations: No full method, evidence table, or locatable example discussion beyond the abstract; cannot carry a distribution claim alone.

candidate_source: Xing Jingwen, 試探粵語母語者習得普通話的負遷移現象——以雙賓句為例
decision_reason: Full text synthesizes several order classifications and exposes disagreements about verb classes, weight, and declining 過, while its own experiment targets Mandarin transfer.
access_and_locator_state: Full 46-page university-hosted PDF verified; relevant review pp. 2-7.
independence_scope: Secondary synthesis; own participant data are not native Cantonese distribution data.
limitations: Do not count cited statements as independent from their primary sources; do not use Mandarin learner errors as direct Cantonese grammar evidence.

query_group: bei constructions; full and non-full forms; ambiguity; corpus counts
candidate_source: Wong, The bei Constructions in Cantonese: A Corpus-Driven Study
decision_reason: Full paper reports a declared all-occurrence analysis across HKCanCor and HKCAC, pattern counts, ellipsis/topicalization classes, non-target functions, and context-dependent ambiguity.
access_and_locator_state: Official landing page and full PDF verified; Proceedings 10(1):95-105; DOI verified. CP021B-SY corrects the canonical DOI punctuation from an erroneous slash after 10.17501 to the registered dot form.
independence_scope: One researcher analyzing two corpora; some data overlap earlier Wong 2009.
limitations: Counts depend on the author's categorization; 1990s corpora; sparse cells are explicitly inconclusive; PDF metadata anomaly is a quality warning.

query_group: gei/bei corpus contrast; Cantonese pattern inventory
candidate_source: Wong, Gei Constructions in Mandarin Chinese and Bei Constructions in Cantonese: A Corpus-Driven Contrastive Study
decision_reason: Full text is relevant for the earlier corpus taxonomy and ordering account; the later 2023/2024 paper explicitly corrects at least one earlier pattern caused by an Excel encoding artifact.
access_and_locator_state: Author-uploaded full text verified; International Journal of Corpus Linguistics 14(1):60-80.
independence_scope: Same author and overlapping corpus family as SCREEN-CP021B-007; not independent replication.
limitations: Any proposition contradicted or corrected by the later paper must carry the correction; do not double-count shared data.

candidate_source: Ngai, Haplology of Cantonese BEI: Evidence in Production
decision_reason: Full study provides a recent production test of competing double-bei/haplology accounts and reports uncertainty from a small sample.
access_and_locator_state: Full 29-page university-hosted PDF verified.
independence_scope: Independent small production study.
limitations: Small, inconclusive sample; literature claim about absence of adult-only corpus studies conflicts with recoverable adult corpus studies and is not adopted.

query_group: lexical GIVE anomaly; VTR/VRT; heavy theme; child and adult data
candidate_source: Lam, Object Functions and the Syntax of Double Object Constructions in Lexical Functional Grammar
decision_reason: Official full dissertation contains locatable Cantonese form/order judgments, heavy-theme alternatives, and corpus-related discussion.
access_and_locator_state: Oxford ORA record and full file verified; relevant sections pp. 146-154 and 193-197 registered for extraction.
independence_scope: Same research program/evidence family as SCREEN-CP021B-011.
limitations: Formal LFG analysis and child-language sections must be separated from adult modern-Cantonese propositions.

query_group: anomalous syntax of GIVE; double object
candidate_source: Lam, Double Object Constructions and Anomalous Syntax of GIVE in Cantonese
decision_reason: Publisher record confirms a peer-reviewed article directly covering the same phenomenon and supplies a citable publication endpoint.
access_and_locator_state: Publisher abstract/metadata verified; Language Sciences 45:71-95; full journal text access-limited.

query_group: double-object versus serial verb; lexical GIVE; beneficiary
candidate_source: Bodomo, Lam, and Yu, Double Object and Serial Verb Constructions in Cantonese
decision_reason: Full paper supplies explicit lexical-give and serial patterns, claimed semantic nuances, and a formal complex-predicate analysis.
access_and_locator_state: Official proceedings PDF verified; 16 pages; proposition locators pending extraction.
independence_scope: Independent formal-analysis team, with Lam overlapping SCREEN-CP021B-010/011.
limitations: Small author-judgment inventory and formal LFG aim; reported nuance must remain source-attributed, not generalized automatically.

query_group: grammaticalization; transfer/beneficiary distinction; category of bei
candidate_source: Chin, Grammaticalization of the Cantonese Double Object Verb [pei35] 畀 in Typological and Areal Perspectives
decision_reason: Full author copy provides explicit examples, transfer/beneficiary contrasts, discourse-completeness judgments, marker omission, and preposition-versus-serial discussion.
access_and_locator_state: Full 34-page university-hosted PDF verified; Language and Linguistics 12(3):529-563.
independence_scope: Independent grammaticalization account citing some earlier double-object literature.
limitations: Examples and judgments do not constitute exhaustive conversational distribution; the grammaticalization analysis is not parser ontology.

query_group: adult input; bei2 double object; bilingual comparison
candidate_source: Wing Shan Angel Chan, The Cantonese Double Object Construction with bei2 'give' in Bilingual Children: The Role of Input
decision_reason: The official abstract establishes a corpus-based input study and reports that the target [bei2-Theme-Recipient] mapping is not consistent in the Cantonese input; no adult distribution table was available in the verified access path.
access_and_locator_state: Official SAGE metadata and abstract verified; International Journal of Bilingualism 14(1):65-85. The prior screen incorrectly named coauthors and pointed to unrelated DOI 10.1177/1367006910371025; corrected DOI is 10.1177/1367006909356653.
independence_scope: Child/input corpus evidence; do not treat it as an independent unrestricted-adult conversational distribution.
limitations: Child acquisition is the primary target; use only abstract-visible input claims until the full coding tables are recovered.

query_group: adult conversational dative variants; optional elements
candidate_source: Wong, Chow, McBride-Cheng, and Stokes, Optional Elements and Variant Structures in the Productions of bei2 'to Give' Dative Constructions in Cantonese-Speaking Adults and Three-Year-Old Children
decision_reason: Official abstract reports 86 parents and 53 children, full/non-full forms, VRT/VTR variants, and greater adult use of prepositional/serial than ditransitive forms.
access_and_locator_state: Cambridge official abstract and issue metadata verified; Journal of Child Language 37(1):175-196; full text access-limited.
independence_scope: Independent conversational sample; possible researcher overlap with other child-language work but distinct author set/dataset.
limitations: Use only claims visible in the official abstract until full tables and coding definitions are recovered; parent-child conversation register.

candidate_source: Chan, Bei2 as a Causative and Passive Marker in Cantonese
decision_reason: Full text with six consultants directly addresses causative/passive analyses sharing the same orthographic form and helps prevent recipient-only overreach.
access_and_locator_state: Official repository PDF verified; proposition locators pending extraction.
independence_scope: Independent small consultant study; not a transfer/dative distribution study.

query_group: natural conversational attestations; all 畀/俾 candidates
